Thank you so much.","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-05-22T19:03:15.951173Z"},{"Name":"Motse","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVOSykMMnpUcfM4ndIPAm02D2ENkWY-jXh5eHVlaOTQLIeUkSw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"After buying the house which was under SCP security, we felt it was appropriate  to continue with them.We made the arrangements that once we move in, the technical team will come to assist us with new set up however it took three weeks with them promising to come but never came as such we decided to go with another active provider on our street. We called the office to alert theme are still waiting, we had to wait the whole weekend without the security cover.

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Vanessa Vrey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJgFlNWgeRE6ATXD0Ep8vXh0k_uEPNFrS1B-YTxL5u18vp31Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I would like to bring to your attention an incident that occurred this morning in Upper Houghton involving one of your drivers.

At the time of the incident, the traffic signal at the intersection was not functioning. As per the South African National Road Traffic Regulations, when a traffic signal is out of order it must be treated as a four-way stop, meaning that motorists must stop completely and proceed in the order in which they arrived at the intersection.

I had come to a complete stop at the intersection and began moving forward when it was my turn to proceed. At that point, one of your drivers drove through the intersection without stopping and then proceeded to behave in an aggressive and confrontational manner from within his vehicle, including shouting at me.

This conduct was both unsafe and unacceptable. In addition to failing to follow the correct procedure at a non-functional traffic light, the driver’s aggressive behaviour created an unnecessary and potentially dangerous situation.

Unfortunately, I was unable to capture the full vehicle registration number, but I recall that the number plate included the digits “22.”

I request that this matter be addressed internally and that your drivers be reminded of their responsibilities when approaching intersections where traffic signals are not operational, as well as the importance of maintaining professional and respectful conduct on the road.

. I trust that appropriate steps will be taken to ensure such behaviour does not occur again.","Images":null,"When":"3 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT25VNE9HczFUbFl3UjJKTFFuZzNTRUowU1haMmIyYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/105984195429596133388/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1773032667137769,"updated_at_unix_micros":1773032667137769,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I would like to bring to your attention an incident that occurred this morning in Upper Houghton involving one of your drivers.

We have been having countless issues over the past months with bad management from the body corporate (which I guess is a separate issue from the security aspect) but the security guards have absolutely no concern regarding the safety of the residents in the complex. The gate and boom gate system never work. A new system was recently implemented and it’s the worst. One of the booms is broken and therefore remains permanently open. To avoid “wear and tear” on the gate, the gate stays open during the day and is only closed at night. When entering the complex, residents are expected to open their window and scan a security tag to open the boom gate and furthermore, if at night,  press the remote to open the gate. The intercom on which the scanner is on, is loose in the pavement that it is poorly bolted into and is leaning over, making it a struggle to reach. The security are constantly sitting and watching me struggle to open, having to drive as close to the pavement as I can and trying not to scrape my rims or hit my side mirror and then having to put my car in park and get half my body out the window just to scan a security tag. By the time I have done this, the gate starts closing. This evening I returned home at 11:25pm, it was raining and due to safety concerns and a 2 year old in the car, did not feel comfortable opening the window and doing all of that to try gain access to the complex (keeping in mind that the exit boom is broken and standing wide open with residents resorting to entering from that side to avoid the inconvenience of this system although I did not do so as I was just following the rules and system. The security walked out of the guard house and just stood there watching and refused to open the boom, posing a hijack risk (another resident was almost hijacked previously at this gate). Eventually after opening, he stated that it is not his job to open the boom and if we forget our tags and cannot open then only will he open. I fail to understand how this makes any logical sense? No consistency as they open for select residents but have an issue with others. Naturally, we got upset and questioned this to which the guard got annoyed and as we drove away, heard him swear at us. My husband stopped and went back to ask why he swore at us and he argued and justified his behavior stating that if we question things and get upset then he can do so. My husband said he would escalate the matter and left it at that. After getting into our apartment, my husband received a call from Vision control room and the security, Edward, who called was shouting and asking why we are harassing his guards. My husband told him that his guard swore at us and explained the situation and asked him if he is aware of the security risk and the boom which is permanently standing open. He refused to acknowledge this, continued arguing and then put the phone down. This is just one of many other security risks in the complex which are ignored by the security. From the very beginning, I noticed a significant difference in his professionalism and work ethic. He speaks fluent English, maintains eye contact, and presents himself with confidence and respect. It was completely unprofessional and unrealistic. As a family with children aged 5, 9, and 12, we planned a special evening out before the school year started. What was meant to be a memorable night instead left my children deeply traumatized.
Just five minutes after arriving at our destination, a heavily armed security officer approached my husband, asking if the vehicle outside was his. He said there was an issue with the car. We were then informed that our vehicle had been hijacked in 2022. My husband immediately clarified that this was our vehicle, we had all the necessary documentation, and it had never been hijacked.
The situation escalated when 14 members of the JMPD, along with two security vehicles, surrounded us. They claimed our car had been used as a getaway vehicle and that my husband was being arrested. The time and date of the alleged crime were when we were on holiday in Hazyview, as confirmed by our fleet company. Despite this, the car was impounded after 4 hours.
During this ordeal, my children were left stressed, frightened, and hungry. My husband, who suffers from diabetes, high blood pressure, and high cholesterol, was unable to take his medication.
The following day, we went to the station to clear the vehicle. It was there that we learned no case existed regarding our car. The problem was due to your security company entering incorrect information into your system—wrong vehicle make, wrong number plate. Your company has had the wrong information for over three years. Instead of resolving the situation, the individual became extremely aggressive and verbally abusive toward me, making false accusations and escalating the situation unnecessarily.

I chose to leave to avoid further confrontation, but the situation took a serious turn when the individual reportedly threatened violence, stating he would return with a weapon. At that point, I contacted Beagle Watch, and their response was immediate. Their presence helped restore a sense of safety, allowing me to return, walk my dogs, and leave the area without further incident.

open_hours: {"Friday":["7:30 am–3 pm"],"Monday":["7:30 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["7:30 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["7:30 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["7:30 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Suné Stevens","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWvv0JRe-fCrVI5Bw_x2Vm7u7nNfE5mTgxgwmUl8P36T8ryUmbU=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A Day I'll Never Forget - GP Tactical Saved the Day

Yesterday started as a nightmare, but thanks to GP Tactical, it ended with overwhelming relief and gratitude.

A close family friend went missing.

I was 80km away at work, helpless. The only other family she had was sitting in Europe, separated by distance and a significant language barrier. In that moment of panic, I made one call—to Mike at GP Tactical.

What happened next was nothing short of extraordinary.
Mike and his team sprang into action immediately. Their professionalism was unwavering, even as my adrenaline surged through the roof. While I rushed home, coordinating between GP Tactical, the foreign family members, and my own family who had started searching, Mike was already working his magic.

Then the call came: "She is safe. I am with her."

Seven words.
Seven words that changed everything.
Seven words that lifted an unbearable weight off my shoulders.
Seven words I will carry with me for the rest of my life.

Thank you, GP Tactical.
Thank you Francois.
Thank you, Mike, and thank you to your entire team.
Your incredible service, compassionate support, and lightning-fast response made all the difference when it mattered most. You are truly appreciated and deeply respected.

I cannot recommend GP Tactical highly enough—and I sincerely hope to work with you again under much happier circumstances!","Images":null,"When":"8 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xoRVlVdGlOMlJGU2pBemJYRjZWRlJuYzJGNFZYYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":5,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113817737010746287651/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1759342390756619,"updated_at_unix_micros":1759342390756619,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"A Day I'll Never Forget - GP Tactical Saved the Day

Our home was silent, stripped of power and water due to an Eskom fault. Suddenly, I noticed a figure at our sliding door. At first, I thought it was my husband checking the windows—until I heard his voice from the bedroom. In that instant, I realised we were not alone. I hurled the milk at the intruder and sprinted for the safe room as gunshots rang out behind me.

In sheer panic, I sent a message to the SDS WhatsApp group. Despite our rural location and the dirt road that usually takes 10 minutes to navigate, SDS Security reached us in just 5 minutes. Five minutes—that’s nothing short of extraordinary. Their swift response not only protected us but also ensured the police were dispatched immediately.

I will forever be grateful to SDS for their speed, professionalism, and courage in our hour of need. They are organised, ready for action, and truly committed to safeguarding their clients. I would recommend SDS Security without hesitation to anyone seeking reliable protection. I have been having issues with the alarm panel installed by the technical team - which they installed a brand new system around the end of November 2024.
First instance the technician that arrived at the premises had no idea why he was even sent to the property to check, which shows that no proper job card was given to him. Upon completion, the technician then decided to blame me for the faults with the keypad and when I told him the system is still under warranty and if it does it again, then I want it replaced with a new on. His response was "no we wont do that".
The system then started giving issues yet again and after demanding that it gets replaced, there was blatant disregard for my request and this was not done, instead they replaced only a PC Board. Not even 3 days later, the system is faulty yet again.
I have been requesting that someone from a senior position contact me to discuss, but it is yet another instance of blatant disregard for requests and nobody from a senior position at TRSS bothered to call. Eventually after back & forth emails, the PR officer decided to call.
Dealing with the TRSS patrol officers & SWAT team is nothing but amazing, but when it comes to the Technical Team, they beyond horrendous.

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Cherize Welgemoedt","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL8wFVVMwcEXbHNuAJT1GMsWaxfwZWfNKpwELNGtXotccPcXw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I would like to express my sincere gratitude and appreciation to MonitorNet for their absolutely exceptional service and incredibly fast response during a medical emergency on 9 May 2026 at Irene Dairy Farm.

My family and I were visiting the farm to spend the day with my mother for Mother’s Day when we encountered an elderly gentleman who appeared to be suffering from a possible stroke. I called for assistance on the Centurion emergency group at approximately 11h30, and within minutes MonitorNet’s control room contacted me, arranged an ambulance, and dispatched a reaction officer to assist us.

A very special thank you goes to Officer Johannes Chauke, who went above and beyond in every possible way. Johannes was calm, professional, compassionate, and extremely helpful throughout the entire incident. He assisted wherever he could, helped keep the elderly lady calm while we attended to the patient, and stayed with us until Netcare 911 arrived. He only left shortly before the ambulance departed.

In a stressful and emotional situation, Johannes represented MonitorNet with excellence, humanity, and professionalism. It is rare to encounter someone so dedicated and genuinely caring.

Thank you to the entire MonitorNet team for your outstanding service. Experiences like this truly make me proud and reassured to be a client of yours. Johannes Chauke deserves the highest recognition for the exceptional service he delivered.

Despite my hard work and dedication as a Site Supervisor at Raslouw Glen Estate and later at Ariston, I was never offered a permanent contract.

Instead, I was asked to sign an UBUNTU learnership contract, which felt like a way for the company to benefit from my skills without committing to me long-term. It's painful to feel like I've been used, but I'm grateful for the experience and skills gained, including my SASSETA Certificate in Safety and Security (NQF Level 03).

I had hoped to grow with the company, but it seems that's not what they had in mind for me. I've decided it's time to move on and find an organization that values my contributions.

Working at EBS has been a bittersweet experience. I've learned a lot, but I've also seen how companies can take advantage of their employees.

I'm proud of the work I did at Raslouw Glen Estate and Ariston, and I'm grateful for the opportunities I had. However, I'm not proud of how I was treated.

I've applied for other jobs, and I'm hopeful that I'll find a company that recognizes my worth. It's tough putting yourself out there, but I know it's worth it.

I've been reflecting on my time at EBS, and I realize that I deserve better. I deserve a company that invests in me and my future.

My SASSETA Certificate is a testament to my hard work and dedication. I'm proud of what I've achieved, and I'm excited to see what the future holds.

I've learned that sometimes, you have to walk away from things that aren't working out. It's not easy, but it's necessary.

I'm not angry, just disappointed. I had high hopes for my time at EBS, but it didn't work out.

I'm focusing on the positive now. I'm grateful for the experience, and I'm excited to see what's next.

I've got a solid foundation, and I'm building on it. I'm confident that the right opportunity will come along.

I'm taking care of myself and prioritizing my well-being. I deserve it after 5 years of hard work.

I'm not sure what the future holds, but I'm excited to find out. I'm ready for new challenges and opportunities.

I've got my SASSETA Certificate, and I'm proud of what I've achieved. I'm looking forward to the next chapter.

I'm moving on, and I'm not looking back. I'm focused on the future and what's to come.

I've learned to appreciate myself and my worth. I deserve to be valued and respected.

I'm not just a former employee; I'm a skilled professional with a lot to offer. I'm confident in my abilities, and I'm ready for the next challenge.

I'm grateful for the experience, but I'm ready to move on. It's time for a new chapter.

I'm excited to see what the future holds and to find a company that values me for who I am.

I'm taking a leap of faith, and I'm excited to see where it takes me. Wish me luck!

I'm proud of myself for taking this step, and I'm looking forward to what's next.

I'm strong, capable, and deserving of success. I'm ready for whatever comes next.

I'm moving forward, and I'm not stopping anytime soon. The future is bright, and I'm excited to see what it holds.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1j0WdpalfixKLEF811BJ3FtpMoinmt8MsLTyo5M1PQeq2e9Hxl-UiUSld_c2PsNcEovZlvV44L9_e9Q5RDy-06ECyhMhBtY2kPP7RkZ3Zv26sT_g_GYtkNfmgLZJ5KqdDSk0P-_zgPuGU=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1rXZt0bd1oR68R18UogzToH3NJ8RXcXrknjub9St2BUOemQjmEmj8uMBlep4fAEF4k3mhMejOWAvZVwR-wEhHwuzAZ-LJXifrs3hgtRsFdfh2_wgeV8yRpy6bgHnt5B3fsrpouQWwjDLJp=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3qSWdOccTwivZFCDv1ioz-l0qk_uLABo0-UcuciZ9wfs9UX14yIR9BuVVYQblvEuY6sygZStAWBhMsbz0d90kfgK99iODnp4ZbeOeW9B64nAzGtKMaphkMHQnFuakg5p4Wm04HVqotnnLU=k-no"],"When":"4 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2pkbFpIQlFNWGhEZG5OWmNEaFpXVTkzYlZsck9WRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":3,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104879224053727317737/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1770880812722863,"updated_at_unix_micros":1770881721184214,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I'm still trying to process my experience working at EBS for 5 years. Every single one of those times I was not at home. Bull proved to be totally out of their depth and incompetent in all three cases. They never came to my property to investigate.

The first time they blamed it on load shedding, even though there should be plans to mitigate that by now.

Second time, they could apparently not gain access to our complex, even though I know for a fact they were loaded on our system and had access.

The third time, today 18/02 2024 their excuse was that a vehicle had been dispatched, but inclement weather was affecting response times. Two hours later they still hadn't arrived . I'll most likely be dead and buried by the time they arrive.

I mean this is not how you run a business!. They are clearly way out of their depth in the sense that they have way too many customers for the amount of people they employ. They clearly need new management, preferably someone with a military background. I don't blame the response team, this is all the fault of management, or lack thereof. Six years of monthly payments (which they never had to wait for, always paid on time) down the drain)

The call centre operators also always answer the phone or call you in a tone of voice suggesting that they are either bored, irritated, or you are inconveniencing them, unacceptable. There is also no manager on duty after hours. In my opinion this is the cause of all these issues. There should be a manager present 24/7.

Bull security don't have a motto for some strange reason. I have one for them, here goes" We are never there for you when you need us the most". Thank you very much!

Just a quick update on my original review.

The people in the control room are definitely more courteous and professional.

I was impressed with the quick response time recently. Considering the above, I have amended my review to 3 stars. Hopefully we can work our way back to a 5 star review.

I would like to give a heartfelt thank you to Phillip, a security officer working at CSI, for the incredible support he gave our family during an emergency on the night of 6 March 2026 just after 23:00.

During the emergency, my son ran to the boom gate to inform security that we were waiting for an ambulance. Phillip immediately stopped, asked my son what was wrong, and personally brought him back to the house to make sure he was safe and that he could assist us.

Phillip stayed with us the entire time while we waited for the ambulance to arrive. His calm presence, kindness, and willingness to help made a very stressful situation much easier for our family. When the paramedics arrived, Phillip didn’t just leave — he also assisted them, truly going above and beyond his duty.

Phillip showed genuine compassion, professionalism, and care for people. It is clear that he takes great pride in his work and serves with passion. CSI is extremely fortunate to have someone like Phillip on their team.

Thank you again, Phillip. Firstly my 74 year old Dad held at gunpoint on a Sunday evening outside his house. When he managed to get to a phone to call me and ask me to notify security, I placed the incident on an area security group (many different security companies).

Needless to say, CSI was not only the first responders within in minutes of the message going out but they also waited outside the house for 3 hours until the police arrived. It’s also important to note that at the time, my Father was not paying them for their services, but in fact a different security company.  The following Monday he moved over to CSI.

Then on a personal experience, I live in a complex which CSI monitor and at 1am on a cold Thursday evening, I hear this determined knocking on my door downstairs.  Phone in hand, and hair everywhere (I was sleeping reallllly good) I ran downstairs with my two just as confused dogs to see what the problem was, only to find 2 strapping CSI gentleman asking me if I’m ok?

Even more confusion from my side and a smile starting to spread over one of their faces, clearly my state of a deep sleep answering his question, lead me to say.. “yes.. why”.  They proceeded to explain how my car door was left open and they were concerned. So yes.. we got home around 5pm and we went inside leaving everything wide open.

I explained this and asked them to close the door for me of which they kindly assisted me with and were on their way again.

CSI took the time to wake me up & check that I was ok and that’s the kind of attention to detail, caring and persistence we need from our security companies.

I’m really impressed and you have two customers for good. [{"Name":"Caren Oosthuysen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXwNW5BQrDAuODOdzKnUztVWXSMvsVCKKPog8rXpAcGpX_4mdKT=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A Huge Thank You to CPG Security! 🚨

Yesterday, while driving near OR Tambo, my tyre burst on the highway. I was left stranded with my valuables and bicycle, feeling very nervous as I waited for my insurance roadside assist – which was going to take 45 minutes to an hour.

Just as I was about to leave my car and bicycle behind and take an Uber, two angels arrived! Within minutes, the CPG Security recovery team was on the scene. They changed my tyre and ensured I got home safely in no time.

A heartfelt thank you to the boss of CPG, who immediately dispatched help, and to Sweiss, Michelle, and Meghan for your swift response, care, and professionalism. 🙏 You truly went above and beyond!

I then notified them that I wanted to go ahead with the installation. Shortly after that I was contacted to schedule the installation date.

We had a gate fitted recently which required custom measurements as a result of an empty window pane which was extended above the door frame. The fitment team arrived on site and had completed the job in such a surprisingly short amount of time.

I was then called for the inspection after installation and was pleasantly surprised. The installation was done professionally and the gate was of good quality. The fitment team was also very professional and friendly. Numerous support calls later, a technician has not been sent, nor has anyone logged into the DVR remotely to download the saved footage and clear the disk. The solution? I cut the power to it for blessed silence and the ability to sleep at night.

It has now been nearly a month and there have been no requests or calls about the non-functioning cameras. The bottom line is that CPS doesn't care about their day-to-day operations nor do they really care about your safety. If the alarm goes off the response for a callback is usually so fast that I do not have time to switch the alarm off. The response vehicles are normally there within reasonable times considering the patrol areas and the activity at the time. The installers and techs have almost always been there at the appointed times.
A few years ago, my sister fell at around midnight and we could not get her up. We phoned them and somebody came out to help us completely free of charge even though I am sure that is not part of the patrol person’s responsibilities.
We had "visitors" Sunday morning and the alarm went off. We received the call and a few minutes later two patrol cars were on scene. They chased after them but lost them due to fence hopping but came back to just make sure we were OK and to enquire if SAPS is required etc. An hour or two later we received a call from the call center just follow up.

[{"Name":"Antonio Moschetta","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXIU2s_c9aum6JOCU5r2pSAYJ9IH0bbq7DnPVNE6Yq87wk8HYXo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Title: Five-Star Excellence in Service – Highly Recommended
I would like to thank Shane and his team from Security Direct for their outstanding work replacing my 16-year-old electrified fence.
The Project: Replacing an 8-strand perimeter fence on a large corner property.
The Experience: The team arrived promptly at 8 AM and finished the entire installation within a single day.
Integrity & Speed: During final testing, they discovered my old energizer was faulty. Rather than leaving me with a broken system, Shane sourced a new unit and his team returned the very next morning to complete the job in record time.
My fence is now fully functional and looks fantastic. I highly recommend Security Direct for their professional service, high-quality products, and very competitive pricing.

Given our neighborhood's history of gunpoint incidents, including both our neighbors being held at gunpoint on separate occasions, I take security concerns very seriously.

Upon noticing suspicious activity near our neighbor's driveway, I alerted AAA Security and CPF members via the group chat. Unfortunately, the response was inadequate. The patrolling member dismissed our concern, and I was forced to contact the call center twice, speaking with Donna Collins, who failed to provide a satisfactory response.

I then spoke with Nadia Stoltz, the manager, who took a dismissive tone and assumed the incident was insignificant, without making any effort to investigate or address our concerns. Her lack of urgency and apparent indifference were alarming and unacceptable.

What's even more disturbing is that I'm not even a client of AAA Security. However, after this encounter, I would not consider signing up for their services, nor would I recommend them to anyone. The way they treat their own clients' concerns is appalling, and I can only imagine how they would respond to actual security threats.

To add insult to injury, it was Graphite Security, a completely different security company, that eventually assisted us and took our concerns seriously. Their prompt and professional response was a stark contrast to the lackluster service we received from AAA Security.

As a neighbor who's witnessed firsthand the serious security threats in our area, I expect a more proactive and responsive approach from security companies. AAA Security's subpar service has left a lasting impression, and I would advise others to explore alternative security options.","Images":null,"When":"a year ago","review_id":"ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNfNS1fUjNBRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/111647665184441299643/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1737306914550396,"updated_at_unix_micros":1737354530706001,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I'm extremely disappointed with the response from AAA Security regarding a suspicious activity incident on Sunday. My elderly father went to this business this morning simply to ask for advice regarding a gate motor battery. He was refused entry immediately, sworn at, and told to “bring the battery” and pay R50 — despite the fact that we didn’t even know whether the battery was the issue. He was asking for guidance, not demanding free service.

What made this experience completely unacceptable was the aggressive and abusive behaviour from staff. While my father was being spoken to rudely and sworn at, another man inside the office shouted “moer hom, moer hom”. This is beyond unprofessional — it is threatening and disgraceful. I am aware they may have had issues with previous customers, but that does not justify projecting hostility onto every person who walks through the door, especially someone with good intentions who has supported this business in the past.

No customer deserves to be treated with intimidation, aggression, or verbal abuse. Based on this experience, I strongly advise others to take their business elsewhere. This company clearly has no respect for customers and should seriously reconsider whether they should be operating at all.

[{"Name":"Brenda De Kock","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWvEYzPBZ5nVNoXSCm6EHXO07_pW7PM72MllAdXqrop8MUkMYbM3g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"You don’t always get a success story… but this weekend reminded us that sometimes things truly do work out.

On Saturday, a watch went missing. We tracked the watch through Apple and realised the watch was no longer anywhere near us. The watch traveled from Centurion all the way to Germiston.

What started as a stressful moment quickly became a coordinated effort.
We contacted the Super Cops team, and from the very first call they were nothing short of incredible. They guided us, supported us, and took the situation seriously — treating it with the urgency and professionalism you can only hope for in moments like these.

For hours, we tracked the watch together, following every signal update, every location, and every possible lead. Their team stayed with us through the entire process, offering reassurance and taking action where needed.

And finally… success.
The watch was recovered safely.

It may seem like a small item, but the relief and gratitude we felt in that moment were huge. Knowing that there are teams like Super Cops who show up, go the extra mile, and genuinely care makes all the difference.

A massive thank you to the entire Super Cops team — your assistance, dedication, and support turned a stressful situation into a positive outcome, and we are truly grateful. 🙌✨","Images":null,"When":"6 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tKYVZVaGZYMjEwWldobk9GZEJkbFoyVlVocGNWRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":5,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113601674376281982404/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1765230155436428,"updated_at_unix_micros":1765230155436428,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"You don’t always get a success story… but this weekend reminded us that sometimes things truly do work out.

Alarm in front yard went off at 2h43. Received a call and asked them to send someone to check.
At 3h11 (half an hour later) no one has arrived. Called the control room and were told they were already here. Said it is untrue as we have been waiting in the lounge for them to come in and clear the yard, for 30 minutes now. Was told They drove by and decided everything was fine as we have dogs. Firstly, the dogs are in the back yard. Secondly, after specifically asking to come check and clear , they never checked on our well-being. If we were being held hostage or forbid, killed, they would not have known. Thirdly, security guard arrived at 3h17 after my call. He was never here and in fact did not even drive by, never mind checked. He claims control sent him to number 51. Called the control room again - the person I spoke to at 3h11, when confronted about lying about them being here, never mind the high risk of the entire situation, says it was, and I quote, a miscommunication.

On 26/12/2024 our alarm went off at night. Asked them to come check. It went off again a few hours later, at 22h29. At 22h38 no one had arrived and we hadn't even received a call, yet, so I called them asking where they are and why no one has called. This is therefore the 2nd time in a few weeks their service failed to deliver.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0et-w-0TkXCKkdFrIN-oUftzHwuZ_-k6nMztV9LxYnLEzNTWamOkMaD1qe_TDSpFYlkYCT7tOchKVmqhryiGffJT4oDk1aU-rnHDXhKTYA_IZRmG_iwq2QxeZq0LbqWQv0RZwNNw=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn32fvonKA1Joaj_tfBWDqDZh-eBScoKxt5JKBwNATvpjPCwy327YpoGJHh6WgetPJ_E_6NzYscLRVexNDsr4X3JZ_nnm_CD6uiXX7RvTq_ToHN4qPJG44ENN3LDOCv2GagRhYuC=k-no"],"When":"a year ago","review_id":"ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURmOWJxYmR3EAE","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":5,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/103651633128400839744/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1736385932258123,"updated_at_unix_micros":1736419262066366,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"Thank you to Jodi for resolving the concerns to our satisfaction.

This is therefore the 2nd time in a few weeks their service failed to deliver.","text_translated":"","published_at":"2025-01-09T01:25:32.258123Z"},{"Name":"Bradley Richards","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUYx9Ks4MGGEKJATjTq_0jRg1XJwBQKAQVtU3dKzSFAF6ALEuS79A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"In the early hours of the morning, just before 1 a.m., our alarm and two panic buttons were triggered — yet Supercops failed to respond. My family was left completely vulnerable while the company I pay to protect us only called after 8 a.m. to ask if “everything was okay.”

Their sales manager later claimed they never received the signals from our AJAX alarm system. However, this doesn’t add up because:

* Supercops’ own call centre confirmed receiving the panic signals during their morning call.
* The AJAX mobile apps on two different phones both registered the panic activations instantly, via the same internet/cellular network Supercops’ system relies on.
* AJAX International has confirmed through their system log that the signals were sent to Supercops.

When I requested Supercops’ system log to verify their claim that no signals were received, they refused to share it.
This was not a minor service issue, it was a life-threatening failure. Trust and accountability are critical in the security industry, and Supercops failed on both counts.

To make matters worse, even after this serious failure, they still insist on charging a full one-month notice when you try to cancel, despite putting my family at risk and failing to deliver the service we pay for.","Images":null,"When":"8 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT21kdFMxODFVbUZwWW0xT09HMXRiMTh0VnpoNmFVRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104521635840238729283/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1759840826723104,"updated_at_unix_micros":1759840826723104,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"In the early hours of the morning, just before 1 a.m., our alarm and two panic buttons were triggered — yet Supercops failed to respond. During December my neighbor phoned mee up at like 01:00, she was feeding her baby when she heard something outside.  They had no security of their own and I told her if she ever had a problem to contact me.  I immediately phoned the emergency room and within minutes the Drunken intruder was caught and removed.

The next is not so easy but I have to thank every single soul at CMS for their assistanc.  My mom had Leukaemia and though she wanted to stay in her Garden Cottage until the end she was moved to my sisters house in Birchleigh North.  Last Friday morning she passed away and we needed to get a Paramedic to confirm her death and complete paperwork.

I immediately called CMS as we have medical response also.  I quickly explained the situation and they immediately sent someone there, even though the address was not even in a area where they were working.  When we arrived there, there was even a Response Vehicle there and they asked if they should send out a Chaplain.

That is absolutely above and beyond what any other Security Providers would do!!

Then came the tricky part, as my mom only paid for her own security and her Cottage was already included with BPCP, we had to transfer the contact to us for the time being which also meant a greater expense for us, as mom was charged as a pensioner, but as neither my husband nor I qualified for the less costly plan.  The first information was provided as we expected, but with all the additional expenses we are facing ( mom's electricity, wi-fi, rates and taxes has always been for free) for the fotceable future, we explained our situation with the company and they granted us the same fee as my mother had paid for the next year.

We are truely as proud and appreciative of everything that CMS has done for us from the day we signed up 19, almost 20 years ago, that it just cannot be described in words.  If their service in our area should ever stop, I'll literally shall be packing my bags and move to a place where I know these people are there looking out for me.

GUYS, if your ever patrolling x3 a d see my front door open, knock an I shall offer you a cup of coffee or a cool drink.  Me casa etc. su casa!

We have been members for almost 20 years  and not once did we have problems with their service or cost.
Once my mom's Car Battery was drained and her vehicle was at the back of our single, multiple vehicle, Garage, thus no way to jumpstart it with one of our vehicles.
I called the dispatcher and two vehicles showed up. The men pushed her car out of our steep yard and 5 minutes later we were on our way to pick up my husband from the Airport.

I approached the guard seated in the passenger seat to ask whether they had a valid reason for using the disabled bay. The response was dismissive and aggressive, with the guard stating that they are “in charge” and can do what they want. I felt uncomfortable and left the situation.

Would never trust them","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-02-23T05:14:19.246377Z"},{"Name":"Shuveny Bower","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXi82gzrel79j2htT4WgyqEhW69H9_qsJccgtTSQfguVHCYCGnvzg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Public Review: Night Guard Security – Northgate Mall Incident, 12 October

This morning my 13-year-old daughter called me in distress from our vehicle after seeing a young boy carrying a visibly sick puppy. I was inside the mall, buying supplies for our animal sanctuary, when she phoned.

As I exited Northgate, I found the child and gently asked where the puppy came from. Before he could even answer, a Night Guard security officer barked across the parking lot, “From the SPCA!” The pup was trembling, barely responsive, and clearly in medical crisis.

I calmly explained I run a registered rescue organisation and simply wanted to help — to get this animal the veterinary care it desperately needed. What followed was nothing short of revolting.

Seven Night Guard security officers surrounded me, shouting, intimidating, and trying to snatch the dying puppy from my arms. I was screamed at to “give it back,” accused of “stealing,” and mocked with, “Who will pay for it?” My explanation that the animal was suffering and required medical attention was met with aggression, not reason.

When a white woman stands holding a terrified, starving, sick puppy — and the men in uniform responsible for public safety turn it into a racial spectacle — it reveals rot far deeper than incompetence. Their behaviour was hostile, threatening, and shameful. One of them even muttered about “pulling weapons” while their so-called “owner” sat nearby drinking alcohol.

Thankfully, my friends from Steadfast Security arrived within minutes, handled the situation professionally, contacted the SPCA directly, and even offered the pup food and water — for which they were shouted at by Night Guard as well.

Night Guard Security, your staff’s behaviour was disgraceful. There was no provocation, no aggression — only an attempt to help a suffering animal. Your team’s unprofessional, violent conduct and lack of empathy are a danger to both people and animals.

If I could rate you zero, I would.
Because safety without compassion isn’t safety at all — it’s tyranny in a uniform.

— Shuveny, Founder, Paws from Heaven Animal Sanctuary","Images":null,"When":"8 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xnME1YYzFWMHRTUjFWTWJIWnhabWRMTkU1SExWRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/107384409205144577565/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1760271184689464,"updated_at_unix_micros":1760271184689464,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"Public Review: Night Guard Security – Northgate Mall Incident, 12 October

Their behaviour was hostile, threatening, and shameful. One of them even muttered about “pulling weapons” while their so-called “owner” sat nearby drinking alcohol.

Night Guard Security, your staff’s behaviour was disgraceful. There was no provocation, no aggression — only an attempt to help a suffering animal. Your team’s unprofessional, violent conduct and lack of empathy are a danger to both people and animals.

If I could rate you zero, I would.
Because safety without compassion isn’t safety at all — it’s tyranny in a uniform.

— Shuveny, Founder, Paws from Heaven Animal Sanctuary","text_translated":"","published_at":"2025-10-12T12:13:04.689464Z"},{"Name":"Andrea Carr","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJxscWJpZuUdDfgmD1d64h-rgKU2-DpP3l5fW5jKLKDH4SuLw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This evening at Parkview the guards put up the barrier at the exit right in front of the car, in front of me. Their professionalism was shockingly low, with staff members yelling at each other across the premises, causing chaos and triggering barking from nearby dogs.

Their attire was unprofessional, featuring flashy gold chains and casual wear inappropriate for a security company. To make matters worse, they played rap music at an excessively loud volume, making it impossible to communicate.

When I tried to address these issues, the response was rude and obnoxious, particularly from the wife of one staff member, who resorted to name-calling and swearing when I kindly asked them to lower the music and stop yelling.

We urgently needed the system put in as we were traveling and I signed the papers as installation would commence the next day. I never heard back from them until 2 weeks later. After my travel was over. Quite inconsiderate, inaccurate, and purely focused on sales or commission of sales rather than the “security” element that you as a consumer require.

This decision has led to ongoing issues, including missing footage from critical incidents, lack of access to recorded footage after just 12 hours, and an overall failure to deliver on their promises.

Despite repeatedly voicing our concerns and even holding a meeting with Maxi’s team, including our CTO, no effective resolution was provided. We are now facing legal implications due to the site being inadequately protected, contrary to Maxi’s assurances. After 25 years in the industry, this is, without question, the most appalling service I have ever encountered from a security company. I strongly advise against trusting Maxi Security with your security needs.
